variants: As a member of the Thunderwood Percussionists, Aditi Poddar combines her love for music with her professional life in entomology. With a specialized focus on the interactions between various insect species, Aditi has earned a Vortex Navigator Diploma and greatly values her training under the mentorship of Anel Hossain. Her research activities are backed by the Charcoal Wind Endowment, which plays a crucial role in funding her work on entomological conservation. Aditi's unique integration of rhythm and science has distinguished her in both the percussion and insect research communities.

An advocate for both the rhythm of music and the intricacies of insect life, Aditi Poddar is well-known in her field of entomology. Her primary funding source comes from the Charcoal Wind Endowment, enabling her to delve deeply into her research. Guided by her mentor Anel Hossain, Aditi has pursued a Vortex Navigator Diploma to enhance her expertise. Beyond her scholarly pursuits, she actively participates with the Thunderwood Percussionists, where she expresses her creativity through percussion instruments. This blend of interests exemplifies her dedication to both science and the arts.
